kept.text:
‼️ The Unscalable High-End Boutique Goes to War

"The artisanal hypersonic. The farm-to-table glide body. The single-origin boost-glide. The kitchen is unsure whether Lockheed Martin is a defense contractor or a bespoke ceramics studio in Brooklyn."

profile_bio.description:
Geopolitics - History, Empires, and War - Macroeconomics and Markets - Music - Photography

English / Italiano

quoted_tweet.text:
The five-second epistemology of the emoji department requesting the Dark Eagle.

The emoji department has requested permission to use a missile that does not yet exist in inventory. The Dark Eagle has been a few weeks away from fielding for thirty-six months. Lt. Gen. Lozano was a few weeks away in March. He was a few weeks away in September. He is a few weeks away tomorrow. The asymptote is the weapon system. The first battery is the limit it never reaches.

Eight missiles. Total. In existence. Hand-assembled by Lockheed technicians. Some of those subcomponents are put together by hand. The seminary uses the word exquisite. The kitchen reads exquisite as the financial disclosure. $2,700,000,000 per battery. $337,500,000 per round. One to two missiles per month, fabricated by a guy with a torque wrench in Huntsville.

The artisanal hypersonic. The farm-to-table glide body. The single-origin boost-glide. The kitchen is unsure whether Lockheed Martin is a defense contractor or a bespoke ceramics studio in Brooklyn.

$337,500,000 per round. The launcher it is being requested to hit cost $50,000. The launcher already moved. The flatbed truck drove behind a mountain during the ceasefire. The math is the analysis. The flatbed wins by driving. The ratio is six thousand seven hundred and fifty to one. The empire is buying a six-thousand-seven-hundred-fifty-to-one trade against a flatbed that already left the address.

The emoji department does not stage the ratio. The seminary does not stage the ratio. The cadet on the couch does not stage the ratio. The kitchen stages the ratio. The ratio is the dashboard read backwards.

profile_bio.description: The Strait at zero. The dashboard vs. the war. The five-second epistemology vs. the ground truth.

kept.text:
xCell 

🇺🇸 San Diego based Firestorm Labs (@Firestorm_Labs) announced $82 million Series B funding led by Washington Harbour Partners (@WashingHarbour) along with Ondas, NEA, In-Q-Tel, Lockheed Martin Ventures, Booz Allen Ventures, Geodesic, Motley Fool Ventures, and others.

"Our Series B accelerates our move into scaled production, ensuring we solve the problem of contested logistics directly at the tactical edge. We aren't following the industry; we are leading it by moving the factory to the front lines. With successful customer deliveries and USAF and Army demonstrations behind us, we are now providing the flexible production capacity needed to strengthen the defense industrial base."
- Dan Magy, Co-founder & CEO of Firestorm Labs

"Equipping the warfighter with mission-critical capabilities when they're  needed most is central to Firestorm Labs' mission"
- Chris Moran,  Vice President and General Manager of Lockheed Martin Ventures.

Founding Team of Firestorm Labs
Dan Magy, Chad Mccoy & Ian Muceus

Learn more >> https://t.co/pB9WVUA0VJ

tweet.text:
Opportunity in Defense stocks? 👀🚀

Major defense names have given back a meaningful chunk of gains, but the underlying environment hasn’t really weakened.

$LMT -1.2% 🔴 (26% off ATH)
$RTX -1.0% 🔴 (19% off ATH)
$NOC -1.0% 🔴 (26% off ATH)
$BA -2.0% 🔴 (13% off ATH)
$PLTR -3.8% 🔴 (36% off ATH)
$GD +11% 🟢 (strong earnings reaction)

What’s interesting here:

• Defense budget trajectory is still pointing toward ~$1.5T range into 2027
• Geopolitical risk environment hasn’t meaningfully cooled
• Multiple contraction looks sentiment-driven. 
•Good time to look into a defensive position at these levels.
